commit.message:
Fix Vercel serverless: move db inside api/_db for proper bundling

Vercel's bundler cannot trace imports from api/ to the parent db/
folder, causing FUNCTION_INVOCATION_FAILED. Moved db files into
api/_db/ (underscore prefix prevents it becoming an endpoint) and
added includeFiles config to vercel.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Zod validation rejecting null values for optional client fields

The spreadsheet sends null for empty fields (email, fiscalRegime, etc.)
but z.string().optional() only accepts string|undefined, not null.
Added .nullable() to all optional fields in the update schema.

commit.message:
feat: Epics 4, 5, 6 — Shopping list, Print calendar, Recipe management

Epic 4 — Shopping List & Carrefour Drive:
- Add /api/shopping-list route with aggregateShoppingList() (unit normalization, rayon sort)
- Add ShoppingPanel component (280px, live Zustand derivation via useShoppingList hook)
- Add CopyFAB + useCopyShoppingList hook (fetch → format → clipboard → fallback modal)
- Add ClipboardFallbackModal for browsers without clipboard API
- Add /api/ingredients route + ingredientMap in Zustand store (unblocks live panel AC2-4)
- Wire ingredient cache load in Desktop + Mobile views on app init

Epic 5 — Print Calendar:
- Add /app/print/page.tsx: A4 landscape weekly calendar (Suspense boundary for useSearchParams)
- Add print.css: @page landscape, print-optimized typography (Playfair Display)
- Add print button (Printer icon) in WeekNav opening /print?week= in new tab
- Add getWeekDateRange() for French-formatted week header

Epic 6 — Recipe Library Management:
- Add RecipeForm component with name/category/vegetarian/prepTime/season fields
- Add IngredientRow component with auto-suggest rayon (RAYON_KEYWORDS + suggestRayon())
- Add POST /api/recipes route (Zod validation, sequential ingredient writes, warning toasts)
- Add RAYONS constant, rayonSortKey(), normalizeUnit(), suggestRayon() to utils.ts
- Fix suggestRayon() keyword ordering: sort by length desc to prevent premature partial matches

Code review fixes across all epics:
- Stable React keys via useRef counter + parallel ingredientKeys state (RecipeForm)
- Suspense wrapper for useSearchParams in print page (Next.js App Router requirement)
- noopener,noreferrer on window.open in WeekNav
- Fallback modal data flow fixed (4 CRITICAL issues resolved in CopyFAB/useCopyShoppingList)

chore: tune JUnit parallel test config for single-JVM execution

parallelism with factor=0.5. Benchmarking showed equivalent performance
while improving Spring context caching and reducing Testcontainers
resource usage.

commit.message:
docs: add RAG poisoning lessons learned to ATTACK_SCENARIOS.md

Key findings from iterating on RAG poisoning attacks:
- Planted data beats embedded instructions for Nova models
- Document replacement beats document addition (eliminates RAG ranking)
- Server-side validation (Lambda REFUND_LIMIT) is real defense, not prompts
- Test assertions must require poisoned-specific content
- Prompt wording matters for guardrail bypass at LOW sensitivity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Require minimum 25-char search text for section/context inference (v20260306-I)

Short anchor text (e.g. "shall", "customer", a single word) appears many
times in a contract. indexOf() finds the FIRST occurrence which is often
in the wrong section — causing comments in section 1.6 to show section 8.2.

Fix: skip _preSection and _docContext computation unless:
- For comments: anchor text is >= 25 chars (long enough to be unique)
- For added/removed language: item text is >= 25 chars
When below the threshold, section is left to the AI (which at minimum
sees the comment content and anchor in its prompt).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[TAB 6] Token-Optimierung: Response-Cache + Dokumentation

- router.py: In-Process-Dict-Cache (TTL=5min) in call_with_fallback()
  für identische Prompts → 100% Token-Ersparnis bei Cache-Hit
- docs/token_optimization.md: Analyse, Implementierungsdetails, Redis-Ausblick
- test_token_optimization.py: Vorher/Nachher-Messung der Token-Ersparnis

commit.message:
feat: show version and build date in all UI footers (#128)

## Summary

Display backend service version and UI build date in the footer of all 3
UIs (server, forwarder, receiver). Format: `Rusty Timer · Server ·
v0.4.3 · Built 2026-03-06`

## Implementation

- Added `GET /api/v1/version` endpoints to server and receiver backends
(expose `CARGO_PKG_VERSION`)
- Inject `__BUILD_DATE__` via Vite build time in all 3 UI apps
- Each UI's footer fetches version on mount and displays gracefully
(fallback if unavailable)
- Forwarder reuses existing `/api/v1/status` endpoint which already
returns version

## Testing

All 3 Rust services compile, all 3 UIs build without errors.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
feat: add Agent Memory Graph — causal knowledge across runs (#38)

Introduces CausalMemoryGraph, a causal knowledge graph that links
discoveries, failures, and fixes as traversable edges across task runs.
Agents can now query historical patterns to avoid repeating mistakes
and identify prerequisite file reads before modifications.

Key components:
- memory_graph.py: MemoryNode, CausalEdge, CausalMemoryGraph with
  O(1) append, file-indexed queries, bounded rotation (max 1000 edges)
- Controller integration: records modification→failure, fix→resolution,
  discovery chains, and security flags into the graph
- Agent integration: planner, implementer, debugger, and security agents
  receive causal memory context in their prompts
- CLI: `glitchlab status` shows memory graph stats when edges exist
- 17 new tests covering all recording, querying, persistence, rotation,
  corruption resilience, and per-agent context building

https://claude.ai/code/session_01V8QmSNMJspPrYXu8jhtyym

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add widget snapshot testing (v1.5.0 4/5)

Implement comprehensive widget snapshot tests using MockTerminal:
- 21 tests covering Block, Paragraph, List, Table, Gauge, Sparkline
- Tests verify rendering behavior, border styles, text content
- Integration tests for multi-widget layouts
- Edge case tests (zero-size, single-char areas)
- Style tests (colored borders, styled spans)

Tests use flexible assertions checking key elements rather than
exact character-by-character snapshots, allowing widget rendering
details to evolve while maintaining correctness guarantees.

All tests passing (600+ total tests in project).

fix: gyroscope permission flow + add debug logs + reduce unmute delay

- Moved _initGyro() from connect() to dc.onopen (after connection active)
- Added _requestGyroPermission() called from user gesture (toggleVoice)
  so iOS permission prompt works correctly
- Cached gyro permission in _gyroPermissionGranted
- Added console.log for gyro debug
- Added unmute debug logs to diagnose mic mute issues
- Reduced UNMUTE_DELAY_MS from 2500ms to 1200ms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Unified DM channels for all agent types (#1851)

* refactor: Rename process_coworker_output to process_agent_output

Preparatory rename — no behavior change. The function generically
streams session output to dm-<name> channels and is not
coworker-specific.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Stream DM output for all agent types (channel leads, forks, lead)

All active sessions now get full execution output (text + tool calls)
streamed to dm-<session_name>. Channel leads and forks retain their
existing topic channel output in addition to the new DM output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Type-aware DM nudge for all agent types

The NudgeChannelLead DM branch now resolves the agent type:
- Project lead: uses headed intercom fallback
- Channel lead: delegates to existing channel lead resume logic
- Fork: logs warning, no respawn (crash recovery handles separately)
- Coworker: resumes from SessionRecord (existing behavior)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Extract is_known_agent_name() for unified DM validation

Replaces inline DM validation with a helper that checks all agent
registries: name_to_session, coworker_records, channel_lead_sessions,
fork_bound_threads, and the project lead name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Click any agent name in web UI to navigate to their DM

Derives clickable agent names from existing DM channels instead of
only the coworkers store. Any agent with a dm-<name> channel gets
a clickable name/avatar in message rows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Sub-agent threading in DM channels and client-side rendering improvements

- Add Progress variant to StreamEvent for sub-agent activity tracking
- Extract parent_tool_use_id from stream events (snake_case for 2.1.70+,
  camelCase fallback) to thread sub-agent tool calls under parent messages
- Fix thread replies appearing inline in DM channels — now consistently
  filtered to thread panel for all channel types
- Extract syntax highlighting into dedicated highlighting.js module
- Improve markdown rendering with better code block and diff handling
- Fix DM channel fork_bound_threads interference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Make channel leads and forks clickable in web UI for DM navigation

Three fixes:
- Separate displayName (visual) from clickName (navigation target) so fork
  messages navigate to dm-<forkName> instead of dm-<parentLeadName>
- Expand agentNames to include coworkers and channel_leads from daemon status,
  not just agents with existing DM channels (selectDm creates on demand)
- Always include project_name in dm_agent_names so the lead's DM channel
  populates when running headless

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Apply biome lint and format fixes to web-app

Auto-fix lint issues (useTemplate, useParseIntRadix, useOptionalChain,
noUnusedFunctionParameters) and formatting (quotes, semicolons,
indentation) across Svelte and JS files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Nudge fork sessions for non-user thread replies and fix fork SendMessage usage

Two fixes for fork session behavior:

1. Fork sessions now receive nudges when any sender (coworker, channel
   lead) posts a thread reply in their bound topic thread. Previously
   only user messages triggered fork nudges, so coworker replies to a
   fork's thread went unnoticed.

2. Update fork_initial_framing() to instruct forks to @mention the
   channel lead instead of using Claude Code's built-in SendMessage
   tool, which routes to "team-lead" — a concept that doesn't exist
   in Midtown's channel-based communication model.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address PR review feedback — 5 issues from code review

1. docs/architecture.md: Update stale process_agent_output() description
   to reflect that it now processes all agent types (not just coworkers)
   and supports sub-agent threading via parentToolUseID.

2. headless.rs: Add #[serde(alias)] on Progress variant fields so both
   camelCase (parentToolUseID) and snake_case (parent_tool_use_id) are
   deserialized into the named field, matching Assistant event behavior.

3. mod.rs: Check persistent_state.sessions in is_known_agent_name() so
   stopped coworkers with SessionRecords (but cleaned-up coworker_records)
   are still recognized for DM channel validation and nudge resumption.

4. channelUtils.js + api.js: Fix getCompletedTaskThreadIds() which always
   returned empty Set because kanban.done contains merged PRs, not tasks.
   Add completedTasks field from tasks with status "completed".

5. stream.rs: Restore tool name summary (e.g. "[Bash, Read]") in message
   content for tool-only DM posts, so TUI users can see tool execution
   activity (terminal renderer only displays msg.content, not tool_data).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix ring-back conference dropping: safety timeout kills connected calls

The 45s safety timeout in _callUserBack checked if phase === 'ringing_user'
to decide whether to kill the target's call. But phase was never updated
from 'ringing_user' to 'connected' when both parties joined the conference.
So 45 seconds after creating the user callback, the timeout would fire
and kick the target out of the conference — killing the line mid-conversation.

Fix: Set phase='connected' in ringback-conference-status callback when
both participants join, and add userJoinedConference guard to the timeout.

fix: 修复评分趋势图等延迟图表因 Chart.js defer 加载导致初始化失败

Chart.js 用 <script defer> 加载，但趋势图/准确率图的 IIFE
在 Chart.js 加载前就执行，typeof Chart==='undefined' 提前退出。

修复：将 F8a 趋势图、准确率方向图、胜率趋势图的初始化代码
包装成命名函数(window.AH.initXxx)，并在 window load 事件中
统一回调，确保 Chart.js 加载后正确初始化。

commit.message:
Fix crash when tapping marker entries (#87)

startActivity() in sendGpxUri() was unguarded — an
ActivityNotFoundException (e.g. when resolveActivity returns a system
app that cannot actually be launched in this context) would propagate
uncaught and crash the service.

Wrap both startActivity() call-sites in
try/catch(ActivityNotFoundException) so that a failed launch falls
through gracefully: the resolved-default path falls through to the
NAV_PACKAGES loop, and a failed NAV_PACKAGES attempt tries the next
entry, with showGpsError() as the final fallback.

Also copy baseIntent before mutating it with setPackage() so the
original remains clean for the fallback loop.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v0.10.3: Auto-reconnect and re-apply RGB settings after sleep/wake

- HIDService listens for WebHID 'connect' events to auto-reconnect when
  device re-enumerates after sleep (triggers full reconnect + settings restore)
- DeviceContext listens for 'visibilitychange' to re-apply saved RGB settings
  on wake from Modern Standby (where USB stays connected but IS31FL3743A LED
  driver IC loses its I2C configuration — known Framework firmware bug)
- Settings are re-sent from localStorage after a 500ms stabilization delay

commit.message:
fix: scope intent keywords and builder dispatch to primary structure only

Intent name was injected as keyword for ALL structures, causing
cross-contamination in multi-object prompts (e.g. "chair" keywords
applied to tree). Object builder also dispatched all structures to the
blueprint-level archetype, so a tree in a furniture blueprint got chair
primitives. Now secondary structures use their own type for both asset
resolution and builder dispatch.

dialogue evidence, real excerpts, and depth-based confidence scores.

- SKILL.md Section 4.1: Stage 1 generation instructions
- SKILL.md Section 5 Step 6.1: Stage 2 enrichment instructions
- Storage: ~/.bot-matcher/handshakes/{peer_id}.json
